title: Salty Crunchy French Chicken Skewers
description: A lowfat, FrenchIndian fusion dish featuring chicken drumsticks coated in a spicy yogurt glaze and panko crunch, perfect for a monsoonseason dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200 °C (400 °F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Whisk yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, paprika, cumin, mustard, garlic powder, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Pat the chicken drumsticks dry, then coat them in the yogurt mixture. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix the panko breadcrumbs with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Thread the marinated drumsticks onto skewers, pressing each piece to coat it with the breadcrumb mixture.
Arrange the skewers on the prepared baking sheet, ensuring they are spaced apart.
Bake for 25–30 minutes, turning once, until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 75 °C (165 °F) and the coating is golden brown.
Remove from oven and let the skewers rest for 5 minutes.
Garnish with chopped parsley if desired.
Serve hot for a crunchy, salty, low‑fat French‑Indian monsoon dinner.
